milestones.title: Foundation & Core Engine
milestones.vision: Build the complete monitoring engine — from ping ingestion to miss detection to alerting — with a functional dashboard. After M001, a developer can create a monitor, ping it from a cron job, and get alerted when the job fails.
success_criteria: Developer can sign up via Clerk and access a protected dashboard
Developer can create a monitor and receive a unique ping URL
Pinging the URL records the heartbeat with timestamp and metadata
Miss detection engine correctly identifies overdue monitors based on cron schedule + grace period
Email alert fires within 2 minutes of a missed ping
Slack and Discord webhook alerts can be configured and fire correctly
Dashboard shows real-time monitor status (up/down/new) and ping history
Cron expressions are parsed and displayed in human-readable format

key_risks.whyItMatters: Cron expression parsing + grace periods + timezone handling is the hardest technical problem. If this doesn't work reliably, the product has no value.
Serverless Postgres may have latency on ping ingestion. Ping endpoint must respond fast (<200ms) to not break customer cron jobs.
The miss detection loop runs on an interval. If Railway's process management drops the scheduler, missed alerts go undetected.

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Cron parser correctly computes next expected ping, grace period window works, and miss detection fires alerts for overdue monitors
Ping endpoint responds under 200ms including DB write on Neon free tier
Scheduler loop runs continuously and recovers from transient failures

milestones.verification_contract: Vitest unit tests for cron parsing, monitor CRUD, miss detection logic. Integration tests for ping endpoint and alert dispatch.
milestones.verification_integration: Real Neon DB connection for ping writes, real Resend API for email alerts, real Slack/Discord webhook delivery.
milestones.verification_operational: Express server starts cleanly, scheduler loop runs and recovers, health endpoint responds.
milestones.verification_uat: Manual walkthrough: create monitor → copy ping URL → curl it → wait for schedule → verify alert fires on miss.
definition_of_done: All slice deliverables are complete and verified
Ping endpoint accepts GET/POST and records heartbeats under 200ms
Miss detection correctly identifies overdue monitors and triggers alerts
Email, Slack, and Discord alerts deliver successfully
Dashboard shows monitor CRUD, live status, and ping history
Auth protects all routes with tenant isolation
All tests pass, ESLint clean, tsc --noEmit clean
milestones.requirement_coverage: Covers: R001, R002, R003, R004, R006, R008\nPartially covers: R009 (infrastructure cost validated locally, not in prod)\nLeaves for later: R005 (billing), R007 (landing page), R010 (free tier enforcement)
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
### S01 → S02

Produces:
- Drizzle schema and query helpers for monitors, pings, alerts
- Monitor CRUD API endpoints (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/monitors)
- Ping ingestion endpoint (POST/GET /api/ping/:slug)
- Clerk auth middleware attached to request context
- Monitor model with schedule, gracePeriod, timezone, status fields

Consumes:
- nothing (first slice)

### S01 → S03

Produces:
- All API endpoints that the dashboard will consume
- Clerk auth session for frontend integration
- Shared types from @cronguard/shared

### S02 → S03

Produces:
- Alert configuration endpoints (CRUD alert channels per monitor)
- Monitor status computation (up/down/new based on miss detection)
- Alert history endpoint for dashboard display

slices.title: DB Schema, Ping Endpoint & Auth
slices.demo: After this: developer signs up via Clerk, creates a monitor via API, and pings its unique URL. Ping is recorded in Neon Postgres and visible via API query.
slices.goal: Establish the data layer, auth boundary, and ping ingestion — the foundation everything else builds on.
slices.sketch_scope: Drizzle schema for users/monitors/pings/alerts, Clerk auth middleware, monitor CRUD endpoints, ping ingestion endpoint with sub-200ms response time. Proves Neon free tier latency is acceptable.

slices.title: Miss Detection Engine & Alert Dispatch
slices.demo: After this: a monitor that hasn't pinged within its schedule + grace period triggers an email alert via Resend and optionally Slack/Discord webhooks.
slices.goal: Build the scheduler that detects missed pings and dispatches alerts — the core value proposition.
slices.sketch_scope: Cron expression parsing with cron-parser, next-expected-ping computation, grace period logic, scheduler loop (setInterval or cron-based), alert dispatch to Resend email + Slack webhook + Discord webhook. Alert preferences per monitor.

slices.title: Dashboard UI
slices.demo: After this: developer can sign in, see all monitors with live status indicators, create/edit/delete monitors, view ping history timeline, and configure alert channels — all in a polished Tailwind UI.
slices.goal: Build the React dashboard where developers manage monitors, view status, and configure alerts.
slices.sketch_scope: TanStack Router for app shell and routes, TanStack Query for data fetching, TanStack Form for monitor create/edit. Monitor list with status badges, monitor detail with ping history, alert channel configuration. Clerk auth UI integration.

decisions.choice: Vite SPA + separate Express API (no Next.js)
decisions.rationale: Owner's expertise is React + Vite + TanStack ecosystem. Separate API enables independent scaling and Railway deployment. No SSR needed for dashboard app.

decisions.choice: apps/web, apps/api, packages/shared with npm workspaces
decisions.rationale: Shared types and utilities between frontend and backend. Single repo simplifies CI and deployment. npm workspaces over Turborepo to keep tooling minimal.
Generous free tier (0.5GB storage, 190 compute hours/mo), serverless Postgres with branching, familiar SQL. Keeps costs at $0 until significant scale.

decisions.choice: Free (5 monitors, email), Pro $9/mo (50 monitors, Slack/Discord/webhooks, 30d retention), Team $29/mo (200 monitors, SMS/PagerDuty, 90d retention)
decisions.rationale: $9 sits below psychological $10 threshold, undercuts Healthchecks ($20) and Cronitor ($20+). Free tier at 5 monitors is more generous than competitors (1-3). 112 Pro customers = $1K MRR target.
Free up to 10K MAU, drop-in React components, JWT verification for API routes, webhook events for user lifecycle. Owner has existing experience.
Free tier (100 emails/day, 3K/mo), simple API, React Email for templates. Sufficient for MVP alert volume.
